requirement: Update demo runbook to cover multi-tenant platform + securities exchange

tasks.title: Comprehensive demo runbook update
tasks.description:
Update docs/DEMO_RUNBOOK.md to cover all new GarudaX multi-tenant platform capabilities.

Read the FULL current runbook (1077 lines) to understand existing structure.
Read these source files to get accurate API routes, request/response shapes:
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_instrument.go (instrument CRUD endpoints)
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_order.go (order submission)
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_corporate_actions.go (corporate actions)
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_reports.go (FRC reporting)
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_settlement.go (settlement cycle)
- src/securities-service/internal/server/handlers_session.go (market session phases)
- src/platform-service/internal/server/handlers.go (tenant CRUD)
- src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/securities.ts (14 securities MCP tools)
- src/admin-bot/mcp-server/src/tools/platform.ts (6 platform MCP tools)
- venues/ace-commodities/config.json and venues/mse-equities/config.json

Changes to make:

**1. Update intro** (lines 1-5):
- Change 'AI Powered Commodity Exchange' → 'Multi-Tenant AI-Native Trading Platform'
- Add: 'GarudaX hosts multiple trading venues (tenants). This runbook demonstrates both the commodity exchange (ace-commodities) and the securities exchange (mse-equities).'
- Add tenant header note: 'All tenant-scoped API calls require the X-GarudaX-Tenant header.'

**2. Update Environment Setup** (section 1):
- Update service table to include securities-service (8089/9089) and platform-service (8090/9090)
- Update Docker Compose to show 16 containers (was 13)
- Add health check for securities-service and platform-service

**3. Update Quick Reference** (section at bottom):
- Add Securities API routes: /api/v1/securities/instruments, /orders, /positions, /settlements, /corporate-actions, /sessions, /reports/frc
- Add Platform API routes: /platform/v1/tenants, /tenants/{id}/config
- Note: Securities routes require X-GarudaX-Tenant header

**4. Add Section 10: Platform Admin Demo** (NEW):
- 10.1 List tenants: curl GET /platform/v1/tenants
- 10.2 Get tenant config: curl GET /platform/v1/tenants/mse-equities/config
- 10.3 Create new tenant: curl POST /platform/v1/tenants {id, name, governance_tier}
- 10.4 Activate tenant: curl PUT /platform/v1/tenants/{id}/status {status: ACTIVE}
- 10.5 Setting tenant context: explain X-GarudaX-Tenant header, show how all subsequent calls include it

**5. Add Section 11: Securities Exchange Demo** (NEW — this is the MAIN demo section):
- 11.1 Create equity instruments: POST /api/v1/securities/instruments with X-GarudaX-Tenant: mse-equities. Create 'APU' (APU JSC, lot_size=10, tick_size=0.01) and 'GOV' (Govisumber, lot_size=100, tick_size=1.0)
- 11.2 List instruments: GET with asset_class filter
- 11.3 Submit buy order: POST /api/v1/securities/orders {instrument_id, side: BUY, order_type: LIMIT, quantity: 100, price: 850.00}
- 11.4 Submit matching sell order: POST from different participant {side: SELL, quantity: 100, price: 850.00} → response includes trades
- 11.5 View trades: Show the trades array in the order response
- 11.6 View positions: GET /api/v1/securities/positions → show qty, avg_cost, P&L
- 11.7 Submit market order: POST {order_type: MARKET, quantity: 50} → instant fill at best price
- 11.8 Cancel order: DELETE /api/v1/securities/orders/{id}
- 11.9 Halt/resume instrument: PUT /api/v1/securities/instruments/{id}/status
- Include sample curl commands with full request/response JSON for every step
- Use realistic Mongolian company names and MNT prices

**6. Add Section 12: Securities Lifecycle Demo** (NEW):
- 12.1 Market session phases: POST /api/v1/securities/sessions/{instrumentID}/transition
  - Show full cycle: CLOSED → PRE_OPEN (orders collected) → CONTINUOUS (opening auction runs, then continuous matching) → CLOSING_AUCTION (orders collected) → CLOSED (closing auction runs)
- 12.2 Announce dividend: POST /api/v1/securities/corporate-actions {action_type: CA_DIVIDEND, instrument_id, details: {dividend_amount: 50.0}}
- 12.3 Process dividend entitlements: POST /api/v1/securities/corporate-actions/{id}/process → entitlements created for all position holders
- 12.4 Stock split: POST corporate action with CA_STOCK_SPLIT, details: {split_ratio: 2.0}, then process → positions doubled
- 12.5 Settlement cycle: POST /api/v1/securities/settlements/cycle {date} → obligations processed PENDING→AFFIRMED→NETTED→SETTLED
- 12.6 FRC reports: GET /api/v1/securities/reports/frc?type=DAILY_SUMMARY&date=...

**7. Add Section 13: Admin UI Walkthrough** (NEW):
- 13.1 Login to admin dashboard (port 3001)
- 13.2 Tenant selector: use the TopBar dropdown to switch between ace-commodities and mse-equities
- 13.3 Platform Admin page: view tenants, create new tenant, activate/suspend
- 13.4 Securities Instruments page: DataGrid with instruments, create instrument modal, halt/resume
- 13.5 Securities Orders page: select instrument, view order book, submit order, cancel
- 13.6 Securities Positions page: view P&L with green/red colors

**8. Add Section 14: Bot Commands** (NEW):
- List all 17 MCP tools with example invocations:
  Securities: list_securities_instruments, get_securities_instrument, create_securities_instrument, submit_securities_order, list_securities_orders, cancel_securities_order, list_securities_positions, list_settlement_obligations, trigger_settlement_cycle, list_corporate_actions, announce_corporate_action, generate_frc_report
  Platform: list_tenants, get_tenant, create_tenant, update_tenant_status, get_tenant_config, provision_tenant
- Show that all securities tools accept optional tenant_id parameter

IMPORTANT: Keep the existing sections 1-9 mostly intact — only update sections 1 (environment) and the Quick Reference. Add sections 10-14 as new content AFTER section 9.

Write handoff to .softhouse/handoff/T1.md
